<style id='block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css'>
/* Large screens (desktops, 992px and up) */
@media ( min-width: 992px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-large-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Medium screens (tablets, between 768px and 992px) */
@media ( min-width: 768px ) and ( max-width: 991.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-medium-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Small screens (mobile devices, less than 768px) */
@media ( max-width: 767.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-small-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}
/*# sourceURL=block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css */
</style>

}

.wfea-card__venue_name {
  font-family: 'Lato';
  font-weight: 700;
}

.eaw-time {
  width: 200px! important;
}

/* Custom carousel navigation button styles */
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev,
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  color: inherit;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -40px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -40px !important;
}

:root {
  --wp--custom--carousel-block--navigation-color: white;
}

.align-content-center{
align-content:center;}

@media (max-width: 345px) {
.footer-copyright p {
font-size:10px;
}
}

@media (max-width: 600px) {

.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -20px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -20px !important;
}

.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-prev,
.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-next {
margin-left: 0;
margin-right: 0;

}

}

.page-id-1186 .is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 24px);
}

.badge-row-hp-cover{
z-index: 2;
position: absolute;
}

.CEO-headshot img{
object-position:top;}

.mtsnb-content{
font-family:"Lato", sans-serif;
}

.page-id-1143 ul{text-transform: none;
}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item{
grid-template-columns: minmax(460px, 1fr) minmax(min-content, 503px) !important;}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item .eaw-content-block .eaw-title{
max-width:40ch !important;}

/* Plus Toggle Style */
.is-style-plus-toggle {
    list-style: none;
    width: 100%;
font-family: "Lato";
padding: 1rem 2rem;
user-select: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] {
padding: 1rem 2rem 4rem;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
    list-style: none;
    position: relative;
    padding-right: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 32px);
    line-height: clamp(30px, 27px + 0.97vw, 42px);
    cursor: pointer;
text-transform: uppercase;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::-webkit-details-marker {
    display: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::after {
    content: '+';
    position: absolute;
    right: 0;
    top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%);
    width: 2rem;
    height: 2rem;
    font-size: 2rem;
    text-align: center;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] > summary::after {
    content: '×';
}

/* Animations */
@keyframes open-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
}

@keyframes close-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] summary ~ * {
    animation: open-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle.closing summary ~ * {
    animation: close-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

/* Content Styling */
.is-style-plus-toggle .details-content {
    padding: 1rem 0;
}

<style id='wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css'>

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text {
			border: 0;
			clip-path: inset(50%);
			height: 1px;
			margin: -1px;
			overflow: hidden;
			padding: 0;
			position: absolute !important;
			width: 1px;
			word-wrap: normal !important;
		}

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text:focus {
			background-color: #eee;
			clip-path: none;
			color: #444;
			display: block;
			font-size: 1em;
			height: auto;
			left: 5px;
			line-height: normal;
			padding: 15px 23px 14px;
			text-decoration: none;
			top: 5px;
			width: auto;
			z-index: 100000;
		}
/*# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css */
</style>

<p>Effective recovery addresses the root causes of addiction, providing lasting, meaningful change by treating both the symptoms and underlying issues.</p>

<script id="wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after">
	( function() {
		var skipLinkTarget = document.querySelector( 'main' ),
			sibling,
			skipLinkTargetID,
			skipLink;

		// Early exit if a skip-link target can't be located.
		if ( ! skipLinkTarget ) {
			return;
		}

		/*
		 * Get the site wrapper.
		 * The skip-link will be injected in the beginning of it.
		 */
		sibling = document.querySelector( '.wp-site-blocks' );

		// Early exit if the root element was not found.
		if ( ! sibling ) {
			return;
		}

		// Get the skip-link target's ID, and generate one if it doesn't exist.
		skipLinkTargetID = skipLinkTarget.id;
		if ( ! skipLinkTargetID ) {
			skipLinkTargetID = 'wp--skip-link--target';
			skipLinkTarget.id = skipLinkTargetID;
		}

		// Create the skip link.
		skipLink = document.createElement( 'a' );
		skipLink.classList.add( 'skip-link', 'screen-reader-text' );
		skipLink.id = 'wp-skip-link';
		skipLink.href = '#' + skipLinkTargetID;
		skipLink.innerText = 'Skip to content';

		// Inject the skip link.
		sibling.parentElement.insertBefore( skipLink, sibling );
	}() );
	
//# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after
</script>

Meadows Behavioral Healthcare | Addiction & Mental Health 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 Contact 
 
 

 

 

 
 

|

 928.260.3568 

 

 
 Admissions 

Admissions

From finding the right treatment program for each individual’s specific needs to paying for treatment, making the most of benefits, and explaining what to expect, our Admissions Experts have answers.

 Learn More 

 Paying for Treatment Who We Treat Length of Stay What to Bring For Families For Referring Providers 

 Inpatient 

 Outpatient 

 Workshops 

 Treatment 

Treatment

Discover the wide range of issues we address, from substance use to mental health and many points in between, as well as the methods we use to ensure not just temporary relief but lasting healing.

 Learn More 

 What We Treat 
 Emotional Trauma Drug Addiction Alcohol Addiction Dual Diagnosis Mental Health Codependency & Control Issues Depression Anxiety Bipolar Disorder PTSD Eating Disorders Sex Addiction & Intimacy Issues Military, Veteran, First Responder Co-Occurring Issues 
 How We Treat 
 Brain Center Detox Types of Therapy Our Approach to Care Military, Veteran, First Responder Specialized Care 

 About 

About Us

Learn more about what set us apart, from our Meadows Model at the heart of our comprehensive approach, to our stellar leaders, industry-leading Senior Fellows, and clinical outcomes data. 

 Learn More 

 Clinical Outcomes Leadership Senior Fellows Locations News & Media Careers 

 Resources 

Resources

Treatment is just the beginning. We also offer a wide range of resources to educate, inform, and support those in recovery, family members and loved ones, and treatment professionals looking to grow. 

 Visit Our Library 

 For Loved Ones Patient Stories Blog FAQs Alumni 

MENU

|

 Contact 

|

 928.260.3568 

 Call Now 

|

 
 

 Call Now 

 

 Admissions 

 + 
 – 
 

 Paying for Treatment Who We Treat Length of Stay What to Bring For Families For Referring Providers 

 Inpatient 

 Outpatient 

 Workshops 

 Treatment 

 + 
 – 
 

 What We Treat 
 Emotional Trauma Drug Addiction Alcohol Addiction Dual Diagnosis Mental Health Codependency & Control Issues Depression Anxiety Bipolar Disorder PTSD Eating Disorders Sex Addiction & Intimacy Issues Military, Veteran, First Responder Co-Occurring Issues 
 How We Treat 
 Brain Center Detox Types of Therapy Our Approach to Care Military, Veteran, First Responder Specialized Care 

 About 

 + 
 – 
 

 Clinical Outcomes Leadership Senior Fellows Locations News & Media Careers 

 Resources 

 + 
 – 
 

 For Loved Ones Patient Stories Blog FAQs Alumni 

 Menu 

 

 

Personalized Addiction & Mental Health Treatment for Lasting Recovery

Find hope and healing with proven treatment created for you, in collaboration with caregivers, providers, and family.

 CALL: 928.260.3568 

 

Celebrating 50 Years of Excellence in Addiction, Trauma, and Mental Health Treatment.

Building a Foundation for Lasting Change

Did you know that completing treatment significantly improves outcomes for individuals facing mental health challenges, trauma, addiction, and other behavioral health concerns? At The Meadows, 88% of inpatients complete their treatment programs, setting the foundation for lasting recovery and long-term improvements in overall health and well-being.

 

of patients complete treatment

Higher Than Vista Industry Norms

 View all Clinical Outcomes 

 

Comprehensive Treatment for Long-Term Recovery

Addiction treatment is about more than just quitting the habit. Substance use is often linked with mental health issues like anxiety, depression, and trauma. Addressing both addiction and mental health simultaneously through dual diagnosis treatment provides more complete and effective care.

Effective recovery addresses the root causes of addiction, providing lasting, meaningful change by treating both the symptoms and underlying issues.

 What We Treat 

 Trauma Drug Abuse Alcohol Addiction Dual Diagnosis Mental Health Eating Disorders Sex Addiction & Intimacy Issues 

Trusted Expertise, Measurable Success

Our treatment programs go beyond symptom management, delivering real, measurable improvements in overall quality of life. The positive changes made during treatment continue to benefit patients long after they leave. With a high overall treatment experience satisfaction rating and proven sustained progress across multiple areas of general well-being, we stand as a trusted choice for those seeking real transformation.

Life Improvements Above Industry Norms

 

92% of patients Report feeling excellent, good, or fair 1 year Post-Treatment compared to 35% at Intake.

 View All Clinical Outcomes 

 

Recovery Starts Today

 

Whether looking for addiction or mental health treatment for yourself or a loved one, we can quickly and confidentially get the process started. Call us today to reach one of our trained admissions specialists. We are here to make the entire process easier for you. 

 928.260.3568 

Our Mental Health and Addiction Treatment Programs

From inpatient care to outpatient support, workshops, and coaching, we offer industry-leading care at every stage of recovery. We support you throughout your personalized treatment journey and offer ongoing programs for alumni to ensure long-term success and foster connection.

 INPATIENT 

 OUTPATIENT 

 WORKSHOPS 

 

Introducing: Unbroken At The Meadows

For Military, Veterans, and First Responders

 Unbroken at The Meadows ™ is a specialized, evidence-based treatment program designed exclusively for active-duty military, veterans, first responders, and public safety professionals experiencing post-traumatic stress injuries and co-occurring substance use disorders. 

 Learn More 

 

 

Traveling to The Meadows for Care

Thinking about treatment away from home? Many patients find traveling to The Meadows provides the privacy, focus, and nationally recognized care needed for lasting recovery. 

 Learn More About Traveling for Care 

 

They changed my life. The Meadows Model works. The staff is amazing and the therapy is the best and most impactful I’ve ever experienced. If you’re willing to do the work, this program can change your life. 

 Curtis 

MBH Alumni

 

Treatment Locations

 ARIZONA 

 CALIFORNIA 

 COLORADO 

 GEORGIA 

 ILLINOIS 

 NEVADA 

 TEXAS 

 View All Locations 

 

If you’re ready to make change in your life, this is the place to be! I appreciated leaving the program with an understanding of my limiting thought patterns and how to redirect them mentally and somatically! I am much happier and better equipped in my life. 

 Andrea 

MBH Alumni

 

How We Treat

Our approach is centered on delivering effective, lasting results. With over 45 years of experience, we combine the proven Meadows Model with cutting-edge tools like our Brain Center and various therapies. Every treatment plan is carefully created to address the unique needs of each patient, ensuring they receive the most effective care possible for long-term recovery.

 Types of Therapy 

 

Abstinent last 30 days

one year post-treatment

 88% of reachable SUD patients at one-year post treatment reported being abstinent the last 30 days .

 View all Clinical Outcomes 

 

 
 

Featured Article

 How Does Dialectical Behavior Therapy Work in Addiction Treatment? 

Intense emotions can be difficult to manage, especially when they build quickly and lead to impulsive choices or behaviors you later regret. For those in recovery, emotional triggers often play a direct role in cravings, relapse risk, and difficulty staying present during stressful moments. Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT) was developed to help people build skills

 Learn More 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 

 

 

 
 

Resources

Looking for more? Find fresh insights from our team and senior fellows, read FAQs, learn about our Alumni program, and listen to our recovery podcasts featuring experts in behavioral healthcare.

 Learn More 

 

 

Taking the Next Step

 

Whether you’re seeking treatment for yourself or a loved one, we’re here with immediate access to the help you need. Contact us today to get started on the path to recovery.

 GET STARTED 

 CALL: 928.260.3568

 19820 N 7TH ST #205 
PHOENIX, AZ 85024 

CALL US TODAY

<style id='block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css'>
/* Large screens (desktops, 992px and up) */
@media ( min-width: 992px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-large-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Medium screens (tablets, between 768px and 992px) */
@media ( min-width: 768px ) and ( max-width: 991.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-medium-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Small screens (mobile devices, less than 768px) */
@media ( max-width: 767.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-small-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}
/*# sourceURL=block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css */
</style>

}

}

/* Custom carousel navigation button styles */
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev,
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  color: inherit;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -40px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -40px !important;
}

:root {
  --wp--custom--carousel-block--navigation-color: white;
}

.align-content-center{
align-content:center;}

@media (max-width: 345px) {
.footer-copyright p {
font-size:10px;
}
}

@media (max-width: 600px) {

.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -20px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -20px !important;
}

.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-prev,
.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-next {
margin-left: 0;
margin-right: 0;

}

}

.page-id-1186 .is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 24px);
}

.badge-row-hp-cover{
z-index: 2;
position: absolute;
}

.CEO-headshot img{
object-position:top;}

.mtsnb-content{
font-family:"Lato", sans-serif;
}

.page-id-1143 ul{text-transform: none;
}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item{
grid-template-columns: minmax(460px, 1fr) minmax(min-content, 503px) !important;}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item .eaw-content-block .eaw-title{
max-width:40ch !important;}

/* Plus Toggle Style */
.is-style-plus-toggle {
    list-style: none;
    width: 100%;
font-family: "Lato";
padding: 1rem 2rem;
user-select: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] {
padding: 1rem 2rem 4rem;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
    list-style: none;
    position: relative;
    padding-right: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 32px);
    line-height: clamp(30px, 27px + 0.97vw, 42px);
    cursor: pointer;
text-transform: uppercase;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::-webkit-details-marker {
    display: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::after {
    content: '+';
    position: absolute;
    right: 0;
    top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%);
    width: 2rem;
    height: 2rem;
    font-size: 2rem;
    text-align: center;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] > summary::after {
    content: '×';
}

/* Animations */
@keyframes open-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
}

@keyframes close-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] summary ~ * {
    animation: open-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle.closing summary ~ * {
    animation: close-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

/* Content Styling */
.is-style-plus-toggle .details-content {
    padding: 1rem 0;
}

<style id='wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css'>

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text {
			border: 0;
			clip-path: inset(50%);
			height: 1px;
			margin: -1px;
			overflow: hidden;
			padding: 0;
			position: absolute !important;
			width: 1px;
			word-wrap: normal !important;
		}

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text:focus {
			background-color: #eee;
			clip-path: none;
			color: #444;
			display: block;
			font-size: 1em;
			height: auto;
			left: 5px;
			line-height: normal;
			padding: 15px 23px 14px;
			text-decoration: none;
			top: 5px;
			width: auto;
			z-index: 100000;
		}
/*# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css */
</style>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-purple-darkest-color has-text-color has-link-color wp-elements-af8e7328841f970d35bd158a447f603e">Expanded Access to Care: We Are Now In-Network with Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona</h2>



<figure class="wp-block-image size-large is-resized"><img decoding="async" width="1024" height="576" src="https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-1024x576.jpg" alt="" class="wp-image-7115" style="width:233px;height:auto" srcset="https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-1024x576.jpg 1024w, https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-300x169.jpg 300w, https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-768x432.jpg 768w, https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-1536x864.jpg 1536w, https://www.themeadows.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Blue-Cross-Blue-Shield-Logo-2048x1152.jpg 2048w" sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px" /></figure>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-column is-vertically-aligned-top is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow">
<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-19e250f3 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="has-purple-darkest-color has-text-color has-link-color has-body-font-size wp-elements-4fe0b849cca692196068d1445748eb68" style="padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)">Effective January 2025, The Meadows is excited to be in-network with Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona (BCBSAZ) for all our inpatient treatment centers in Arizona. This partnership also provides nationwide coverage through the BlueCard Program, bringing our trusted trauma, addiction, and mental health care within reach.</p>



<p class="has-purple-darkest-color has-text-color has-link-color has-body-font-size wp-elements-ba1402fb96725e47eb8e376a0f4add76" style="padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)">In addition to our Arizona inpatient facilities, many of our programs, including The Meadows Outpatient Center and The Meadows Texas, are already in-network with Blue Cross Blue Shield. With this expanded partnership, we’re reaffirming our commitment to making life-changing treatment more accessible than ever.</p>



<p class="has-purple-darkest-color has-text-color has-link-color has-body-font-size wp-elements-31fd6c4fab7894214fa6ced2adb8901c" style="padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)">Learn how your BCBSAZ coverage can help you begin your journey to healing. </p>



<p class="has-purple-darkest-color has-text-color has-link-color has-body-font-size wp-elements-2e1b7932ef46ebe259f604652607de38" style="padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--40)"><strong>Contact us today to get started:</strong></p>

<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-d2d7b3b8 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading wp-container-content-463bd4c8">How We Work with Your Insurance Provider</h2>



<p class="wp-container-content-463bd4c8">Meadows Behavioral Healthcare is in-network with most major insurance providers and many smaller ones. We also work with out-of-network plans. Every insurance plan is different, and not all MBH programs work with the same providers.</p>



<p>To help you get the most coverage, we ask for your insurance information upfront. Your insurance company will decide what care they will cover based on your plan and current needs. They might approve all or part of your stay.</p>

<div class="wp-block-columns fifty-fifty is-style-stack-backwards has-plum-medium-color has-chalk-light-background-color has-text-color has-background has-link-color wp-elements-2e0879398f8a7648454d2a07f47e2155 is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-01e9b6bf wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0">

<div class="wp-block-group is-content-justification-right is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-8d75f03a wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Maximize Coverage for Military and Veterans</h2>



<p>MBH is proud to be a TRICARE provider, offering behavioral health and substance abuse services to active-duty military, veterans, and their families in the TRICARE West Region. We also treat veterans through the VA MISSION Act.</p>

<hr class="wp-block-separator has-text-color has-chalk-color has-alpha-channel-opacity has-chalk-background-color has-background is-style-narrowed"/>



<p class="has-text-align-center has-stark-color has-text-color">Our focus is on helping you get the best treatment that works for your life. If insurance doesn’t cover everything, our financial coordinators will guide you through your options and create a plan that works for you.</p>



<p class="has-text-align-center has-stark-color has-text-color">We also offer financing through CareCredit, a flexible way to help manage treatment costs. CareCredit can be used for any Meadows Behavioral Healthcare treatment, including workshops (excluding lodging).</p>

<script id="wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after">
	( function() {
		var skipLinkTarget = document.querySelector( 'main' ),
			sibling,
			skipLinkTargetID,
			skipLink;

		// Early exit if a skip-link target can't be located.
		if ( ! skipLinkTarget ) {
			return;
		}

		/*
		 * Get the site wrapper.
		 * The skip-link will be injected in the beginning of it.
		 */
		sibling = document.querySelector( '.wp-site-blocks' );

		// Early exit if the root element was not found.
		if ( ! sibling ) {
			return;
		}

		// Get the skip-link target's ID, and generate one if it doesn't exist.
		skipLinkTargetID = skipLinkTarget.id;
		if ( ! skipLinkTargetID ) {
			skipLinkTargetID = 'wp--skip-link--target';
			skipLinkTarget.id = skipLinkTargetID;
		}

		// Create the skip link.
		skipLink = document.createElement( 'a' );
		skipLink.classList.add( 'skip-link', 'screen-reader-text' );
		skipLink.id = 'wp-skip-link';
		skipLink.href = '#' + skipLinkTargetID;
		skipLink.innerText = 'Skip to content';

		// Inject the skip link.
		sibling.parentElement.insertBefore( skipLink, sibling );
	}() );
	
//# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after
</script>

 928.260.3568 

 

 
 Admissions 

Admissions

From finding the right treatment program for each individual’s specific needs to paying for treatment, making the most of benefits, and explaining what to expect, our Admissions Experts have answers.

 Learn More

Treatment

Discover the wide range of issues we address, from substance use to mental health and many points in between, as well as the methods we use to ensure not just temporary relief but lasting healing.

 Learn More 

 What We Treat 
 Emotional Trauma Drug Addiction Alcohol Addiction Dual Diagnosis Mental Health Codependency & Control Issues Depression Anxiety Bipolar Disorder PTSD Eating Disorders Sex Addiction & Intimacy Issues Military, Veteran, First Responder Co-Occurring Issues 
 How We Treat 
 Brain Center Detox Types of Therapy Our Approach to Care Military, Veteran, First Responder Specialized Care 

 About 

About Us

Learn more about what set us apart, from our Meadows Model at the heart of our comprehensive approach, to our stellar leaders, industry-leading Senior Fellows, and clinical outcomes data. 

 Learn More 

 Clinical Outcomes Leadership Senior Fellows Locations News & Media Careers

Resources

Treatment is just the beginning. We also offer a wide range of resources to educate, inform, and support those in recovery, family members and loved ones, and treatment professionals looking to grow.

Understand Your Insurance Coverage, Financing, and Payment Options

We’re in-network with most major insurers, offer flexible financing, and help patients find the best financial support available.

 Get Personalized Help 

Fast and Easy Insurance Verification

 

Our team is here to simplify the process and get you the most coverage possible. We’ll work with you to quickly verify your insurance coverage and help determine the next steps in your treatment plan, ensuring a smooth and efficient start to your recovery.

 Get Started

Expanded Access to Care: We Are Now In-Network with Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona

 

Effective January 2025, The Meadows is excited to be in-network with Blue Cross Blue Shield of Arizona (BCBSAZ) for all our inpatient treatment centers in Arizona. This partnership also provides nationwide coverage through the BlueCard Program, bringing our trusted trauma, addiction, and mental health care within reach.

In addition to our Arizona inpatient facilities, many of our programs, including The Meadows Outpatient Center and The Meadows Texas, are already in-network with Blue Cross Blue Shield. With this expanded partnership, we’re reaffirming our commitment to making life-changing treatment more accessible than ever.

Learn how your BCBSAZ coverage can help you begin your journey to healing. 

 Contact us today to get started: 

 928.260.3568 

 More Info 

 

How We Work with Your Insurance Provider

Meadows Behavioral Healthcare is in-network with most major insurance providers and many smaller ones. We also work with out-of-network plans. Every insurance plan is different, and not all MBH programs work with the same providers.

To help you get the most coverage, we ask for your insurance information upfront. Your insurance company will decide what care they will cover based on your plan and current needs. They might approve all or part of your stay.

Maximize Coverage for Military and Veterans

MBH is proud to be a TRICARE provider, offering behavioral health and substance abuse services to active-duty military, veterans, and their families in the TRICARE West Region. We also treat veterans through the VA MISSION Act.

 CaLl: 928.260.3568 

 

Flexible Financing Options

 

Our focus is on helping you get the best treatment that works for your life. If insurance doesn’t cover everything, our financial coordinators will guide you through your options and create a plan that works for you.

We also offer financing through CareCredit, a flexible way to help manage treatment costs. CareCredit can be used for any Meadows Behavioral Healthcare treatment, including workshops (excluding lodging).

 Apply Through carecredit.com 

 

Learn More About Insurance and Financing

 

We’re here to help you understand your coverage and available payment options. Contact us today to receive personalized support and take the next step toward treatment.

 Get Personalized Help 

 CALL: 928.260.3568

<style id='block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css'>
/* Large screens (desktops, 992px and up) */
@media ( min-width: 992px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-large-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Medium screens (tablets, between 768px and 992px) */
@media ( min-width: 768px ) and ( max-width: 991.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-medium-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Small screens (mobile devices, less than 768px) */
@media ( max-width: 767.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-small-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}
/*# sourceURL=block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css */
</style>

.wfea-card__venue_name {
  font-family: 'Lato';
  font-weight: 700;
}

.eaw-time {
  width: 200px! important;
}

/* Custom carousel navigation button styles */
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev,
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  color: inherit;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -40px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -40px !important;
}

:root {
  --wp--custom--carousel-block--navigation-color: white;
}

.align-content-center{
align-content:center;}

@media (max-width: 345px) {
.footer-copyright p {
font-size:10px;
}
}

@media (max-width: 600px) {

.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -20px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -20px !important;
}

.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-prev,
.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-next {
margin-left: 0;
margin-right: 0;

}

}

.page-id-1186 .is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 24px);
}

.badge-row-hp-cover{
z-index: 2;
position: absolute;
}

.CEO-headshot img{
object-position:top;}

.mtsnb-content{
font-family:"Lato", sans-serif;
}

.page-id-1143 ul{text-transform: none;
}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item{
grid-template-columns: minmax(460px, 1fr) minmax(min-content, 503px) !important;}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item .eaw-content-block .eaw-title{
max-width:40ch !important;}

/* Plus Toggle Style */
.is-style-plus-toggle {
    list-style: none;
    width: 100%;
font-family: "Lato";
padding: 1rem 2rem;
user-select: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] {
padding: 1rem 2rem 4rem;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
    list-style: none;
    position: relative;
    padding-right: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 32px);
    line-height: clamp(30px, 27px + 0.97vw, 42px);
    cursor: pointer;
text-transform: uppercase;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::-webkit-details-marker {
    display: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::after {
    content: '+';
    position: absolute;
    right: 0;
    top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%);
    width: 2rem;
    height: 2rem;
    font-size: 2rem;
    text-align: center;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] > summary::after {
    content: '×';
}

/* Animations */
@keyframes open-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
}

@keyframes close-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] summary ~ * {
    animation: open-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle.closing summary ~ * {
    animation: close-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

/* Content Styling */
.is-style-plus-toggle .details-content {
    padding: 1rem 0;
}

<style id='wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css'>

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text {
			border: 0;
			clip-path: inset(50%);
			height: 1px;
			margin: -1px;
			overflow: hidden;
			padding: 0;
			position: absolute !important;
			width: 1px;
			word-wrap: normal !important;
		}

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text:focus {
			background-color: #eee;
			clip-path: none;
			color: #444;
			display: block;
			font-size: 1em;
			height: auto;
			left: 5px;
			line-height: normal;
			padding: 15px 23px 14px;
			text-decoration: none;
			top: 5px;
			width: auto;
			z-index: 100000;
		}
/*# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css */
</style>

<h1 class="wp-block-heading is-style-pulse-in-h1 has-heading-1-font-size">Dual Diagnosis Treatment</h1>



<div class="wp-block-group is-content-justification-left is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-74cbbf86 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="has-hero-subtitle-font-size" style="font-style:normal;font-weight:400">Take control of your mental health and addiction with comprehensive care for co-occurring conditions.</p>



<div class="wp-block-buttons is-layout-flex wp-block-buttons-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-button is-style-outline-chalk"><a class="wp-block-button__link wp-element-button" href="https://www.themeadows.com/contact/">Get Started</a></div>

<div class="wp-block-group alignwide has-chalk-light-background-color has-background is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-88c27b7c wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">

<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-749858fd wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center">Dual Diagnosis: Treating Addiction and Mental Health Together</h2>
</div>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-text-color has-desert-color has-alpha-channel-opacity has-desert-background-color has-background is-style-narrowed"/>



<p class="has-text-align-center">At our dual diagnosis treatment facilities, we offer a supportive environment to help you address both mental health disorders and substance use issues. Our programs are designed to treat both conditions simultaneously, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. With our integrated approach, you’ll develop the tools to manage both mental health challenges and addiction, creating a more stable and fulfilling future.</p>
</div>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-columns fifty-fifty is-style-stack-backwards has-plum-medium-color has-chalk-background-color has-text-color has-background has-link-color wp-elements-5dc900fcff096083b923fcbd291b5de7 is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-b43e120a wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0">

<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Personalized Care, Lasting Recovery</h2>



<p>Our approach to dual diagnosis treatment focuses on creating personalized care plans that address both your mental health disorder and addiction. We treat the whole person, recognizing how mental health and addiction influence one another. This ensures lasting recovery by addressing the root causes of both conditions and equipping you with the tools to manage them effectively.</p>

<div class="wp-block-query is-layout-flow wp-block-query-is-layout-flow"><ul id="6" class="wp-block-post-template is-layout-flow wp-block-post-template-is-layout-flow"><li class="wp-block-post post-8139 faq type-faq status-publish hentry faq-category-what-we-treat">

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" style="text-transform:none">Dual Diagnosis Treatment FAQ</h2>


<div style="text-transform:none;" class="entry-content wp-block-post-content is-layout-flow wp-block-post-content-is-layout-flow">
<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What is dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p><a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/dual-diagnosis/">Dual Diagnosis</a> is the <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/blog/why-are-co-occurring-disorders-so-common/">co-occurring presence of both a substance use disorder and a mental health condition</a>. This might include combinations like depression and alcohol use disorder, anxiety and prescription drug misuse, or <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/blog/30-things-you-need-to-know-about-trauma-and-ptsd-ptsd-awareness-month/">PTSD</a> and opioid addiction. These conditions often interact and reinforce one another, making professional treatment essential. </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Which typically comes first – mental health concerns or substance use?</summary>
<p>It varies from person to person. Some individuals use substances to cope with underlying <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/mental-health/">mental health</a> struggles, while others develop mental health symptoms as a result of prolonged substance use. The most important thing is recognizing the connection and ensuring both are addressed in treatment for long-term healing and recovery.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Can you treat mental health and substance use at the same time?</summary>
<p>Absolutely. Treating both conditions simultaneously, rather than sequentially, improves outcomes and reduces the risk of relapse. At The Meadows, our programs are designed to treat both conditions at the same time, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>How do I know if I or a loved one is struggling with addiction and mental health simultaneously?</summary>
<p>If you notice persistent emotional distress alongside substance use, such as mood swings, depression, anxiety, isolation, or impulsive behavior, it may be a dual diagnosis. Warning signs also include using substances to cope with emotions, needing more to achieve the same effect, or being unable to stop despite negative consequences. To learn more and see if your loved one could be struggling with a dual diagnosis, call our team at <a href="tel:928.260.3568">928.260.3568.</a></p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What are the symptoms and signs of someone struggling with a dual diagnosis? </summary>
<p>A proper evaluation is important to understand the full picture; however, common signs in those struggling with a dual diagnosis include: </p>



<p>Denial or secrecy about substance use </p>



<p>Changes in mood or behavior (irritability, anxiety, depression)</p>



<p>Difficulty managing daily responsibilities </p>



<p>Withdrawing from loved ones </p>



<p>Substance use that interferes with relationships or work </p>



<p>Trouble sleeping or concentrating </p>



<p>Risk-taking or impulsive behavior </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What kind of treatment works best for dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p>The most effective treatment for dual diagnosis can vary from person to person, but integrated care that addresses both the mental health condition and the substance use disorder is important. <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/inpatient/">Inpatient</a>, <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/outpatient/">outpatient</a>, and <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/workshops/">workshops</a> are all different programs that can help in the recovery of dual diagnosis.  A <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/types-of-therapy/">blend of therapies</a> is often best because it treats the whole person, addressing emotional, physical, and behavioral needs together for more lasting recovery.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>How can I support a loved one struggling with their dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p>Supporting <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/guidance-for-families-and-loved-ones/">a loved one</a> struggling with dual diagnosis starts with listening and letting them know they are not alone. Educate yourself about what they are going through so you can better understand what they are experiencing. It is very important to also take care of your own well-being so you can offer consistent, compassionate support.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Does The Meadows provide post-treatment recovery support? </summary>
<p>At The Meadows we know that recovery doesn’t end when treatment does. The Meadows offers several different options, including step-down programs after <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/inpatient/">inpatient treatment</a> through our <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/outpatient/">outpatient programs</a> with locations across the country. Additionally, our alumni program, <a href="https://mbhonward.com/">Onward</a>, is open to any individual who has gone through one of our programs. Onward provides individuals with a supportive community committed to ongoing resources and empowerment, knowing that recovery is a lifelong journey.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Does treatment for dual diagnosis include family involvement and support? </summary>
<p>Family or friend involvement is crucial to the healing process for dual diagnosis. In our programs, we offer a variety of <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/guidance-for-families-and-loved-ones/">resources for family members</a>, including educational and communication tools, as well as, in some cases, onsite workshops. The decision to attend a family program is guided by several factors, including the length of stay, clinical appropriateness, patient desire, and others. Once admitted, our team members will work with the patient and their family to determine the next steps. </p>
</details>
</div>
</li></ul>

</div>

</div>

<script id="wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after">
	( function() {
		var skipLinkTarget = document.querySelector( 'main' ),
			sibling,
			skipLinkTargetID,
			skipLink;

		// Early exit if a skip-link target can't be located.
		if ( ! skipLinkTarget ) {
			return;
		}

		/*
		 * Get the site wrapper.
		 * The skip-link will be injected in the beginning of it.
		 */
		sibling = document.querySelector( '.wp-site-blocks' );

		// Early exit if the root element was not found.
		if ( ! sibling ) {
			return;
		}

		// Get the skip-link target's ID, and generate one if it doesn't exist.
		skipLinkTargetID = skipLinkTarget.id;
		if ( ! skipLinkTargetID ) {
			skipLinkTargetID = 'wp--skip-link--target';
			skipLinkTarget.id = skipLinkTargetID;
		}

		// Create the skip link.
		skipLink = document.createElement( 'a' );
		skipLink.classList.add( 'skip-link', 'screen-reader-text' );
		skipLink.id = 'wp-skip-link';
		skipLink.href = '#' + skipLinkTargetID;
		skipLink.innerText = 'Skip to content';

		// Inject the skip link.
		sibling.parentElement.insertBefore( skipLink, sibling );
	}() );
	
//# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after
</script>

Dual Diagnosis Treatment Center | Meadows Behavioral Healthcare 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 

 

 

 
 
 

 
 Contact 
 
 

 

 

 
 

|

 928.260.3568 

 

 
 Admissions 

Admissions

From finding the right treatment program for each individual’s specific needs to paying for treatment, making the most of benefits, and explaining what to expect, our Admissions Experts have answers.

 Learn More 

 Paying for Treatment Who We Treat Length of Stay What to Bring For Families For Referring Providers 

 Inpatient 

 Outpatient

Treatment

Discover the wide range of issues we address, from substance use to mental health and many points in between, as well as the methods we use to ensure not just temporary relief but lasting healing.

 Learn More 

 What We Treat 
 Emotional Trauma Drug Addiction Alcohol Addiction Dual Diagnosis Mental Health Codependency & Control Issues Depression Anxiety Bipolar Disorder PTSD Eating Disorders Sex Addiction & Intimacy Issues Military, Veteran, First Responder Co-Occurring Issues 
 How We Treat 
 Brain Center Detox Types of Therapy Our Approach to Care Military, Veteran, First Responder Specialized Care 

 About 

About Us

Learn more about what set us apart, from our Meadows Model at the heart of our comprehensive approach, to our stellar leaders, industry-leading Senior Fellows, and clinical outcomes data. 

 Learn More 

 Clinical Outcomes Leadership Senior Fellows Locations News & Media Careers 

 Resources 

Resources

Treatment is just the beginning. We also offer a wide range of resources to educate, inform, and support those in recovery, family members and loved ones, and treatment professionals looking to grow. 

 Visit Our Library 

 For Loved Ones Patient Stories Blog FAQs Alumni 

MENU

|

 Contact 

|

 928.260.3568 

 Call Now 

|

 
 

 Call Now 

 

 Admissions

 Resources 

 + 
 – 
 

 For Loved Ones Patient Stories Blog FAQs Alumni 

 Menu 

 

 

 

 

 Treatment 
 

Dual Diagnosis Treatment

Take control of your mental health and addiction with comprehensive care for co-occurring conditions.

 Get Started 

Dual Diagnosis: Treating Addiction and Mental Health Together

 

At our dual diagnosis treatment facilities, we offer a supportive environment to help you address both mental health disorders and substance use issues. Our programs are designed to treat both conditions simultaneously, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. With our integrated approach, you’ll develop the tools to manage both mental health challenges and addiction, creating a more stable and fulfilling future.

Personalized Care, Lasting Recovery

Our approach to dual diagnosis treatment focuses on creating personalized care plans that address both your mental health disorder and addiction. We treat the whole person, recognizing how mental health and addiction influence one another. This ensures lasting recovery by addressing the root causes of both conditions and equipping you with the tools to manage them effectively.

 

Inpatient Dual Diagnosis Treatment

Inpatient Dual diagnosis Treatment

 

Our inpatient dual diagnosis treatment centers provide intensive, daily support in a structured environment for co-occurring substance abuse and mental health conditions. This option is ideal for those who need focused care to start their recovery journey.

 Explore Inpatient 

Our Dual Diagnosis residential Treatment Centers

 

 

 The Meadows Arizona 

 The Meadows Texas 

 The Meadows Malibu 

 Claudia Black Young Adult Center 

 Willow House for Women 

 Gentle Path for Men 

 

Outpatient Dual Diagnosis Treatment

Outpatient Dual diagnosis Treatment near you

 

Receive expert support for dual diagnosis mental health treatment while continuing with your daily responsibilities, ensuring you stay on track toward recovery. Explore our 8-week intensive outpatient programs.

 Explore Outpatient 

Outpatient Dual Diagnosis Treatment Centers

 

 

 Scottsdale, AZ 

 Silicon Valley, CA 

 Chicago, IL 

 Las Vegas, NV 

 Houston, TX 

 Denver, CO 

 Dallas, TX 

 Atlanta, GA 

 Austin, TX 

 Virtual IOP 

 

How We Treat Co-Occurring Conditions

Brain Center

 

Our Brain Center uses advanced tools to improve brain function, essential for treating both addiction and mental health disorders. These tools help reduce cravings, manage symptoms, and enhance your ability to cope with stressors contributing to both conditions.

 Learn More 

Types of Therapy

 

Each dual diagnosis treatment plan integrates a variety of therapies to address both your mental health and addiction challenges. This combined approach promotes healing on multiple levels, helping you achieve mental clarity and long-term sobriety.

 Learn More 

The Meadows Model

 

At our dual diagnosis treatment centers, we use the Meadows Model to uncover the emotional and psychological patterns that fuel both mental health disorders and addiction. This approach helps you break free from harmful cycles, allowing you to regain control of your life and achieve lasting recovery.

 Learn More 

 

I highly recommend the Meadows for mental health and trauma treatment. If you are ready to make a change in your life, this is the place to be!

 Andrea 

MHB Alumni

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 

 
 
 
Meet Rachel

 
 

 Addiction 
 Mental Health 
 Trauma 
 

 
 
Finding Strength in Treatment after Years of Sobriety

 
 
When long-standing patterns of depression, isolation, and compulsive behavior resurfaced, Rachel entered inpatient treatment at The Meadows, even though it meant returning to care on her five-year sobriety anniversary. Initially overwhelmed and ashamed, she quickly found comfort in the compassion of staff and peers. Through engaging with Pia Mellody’s Developmental Model of Immaturity, reconnecting with spirituality, and rediscovering joy through connection and play, Rachel began to rebuild a sense of self-worth.

 
 
 Hear Rachel's Story + 
 
 
 
 

 

 

Dual Diagnosis Treatment FAQ

 What is dual diagnosis? 

 Dual Diagnosis is the co-occurring presence of both a substance use disorder and a mental health condition . This might include combinations like depression and alcohol use disorder, anxiety and prescription drug misuse, or PTSD and opioid addiction. These conditions often interact and reinforce one another, making professional treatment essential. 

 

 Which typically comes first – mental health concerns or substance use? 

It varies from person to person. Some individuals use substances to cope with underlying mental health struggles, while others develop mental health symptoms as a result of prolonged substance use. The most important thing is recognizing the connection and ensuring both are addressed in treatment for long-term healing and recovery.

 

 Can you treat mental health and substance use at the same time? 

Absolutely. Treating both conditions simultaneously, rather than sequentially, improves outcomes and reduces the risk of relapse. At The Meadows, our programs are designed to treat both conditions at the same time, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. 

 

 How do I know if I or a loved one is struggling with addiction and mental health simultaneously? 

If you notice persistent emotional distress alongside substance use, such as mood swings, depression, anxiety, isolation, or impulsive behavior, it may be a dual diagnosis. Warning signs also include using substances to cope with emotions, needing more to achieve the same effect, or being unable to stop despite negative consequences. To learn more and see if your loved one could be struggling with a dual diagnosis, call our team at 928.260.3568. 

 

 What are the symptoms and signs of someone struggling with a dual diagnosis? 

A proper evaluation is important to understand the full picture; however, common signs in those struggling with a dual diagnosis include: 

Denial or secrecy about substance use 

Changes in mood or behavior (irritability, anxiety, depression)

Difficulty managing daily responsibilities 

Withdrawing from loved ones 

Substance use that interferes with relationships or work 

Trouble sleeping or concentrating 

Risk-taking or impulsive behavior 

 

 What kind of treatment works best for dual diagnosis? 

The most effective treatment for dual diagnosis can vary from person to person, but integrated care that addresses both the mental health condition and the substance use disorder is important. Inpatient , outpatient , and workshops are all different programs that can help in the recovery of dual diagnosis. A blend of therapies is often best because it treats the whole person, addressing emotional, physical, and behavioral needs together for more lasting recovery.

 

 How can I support a loved one struggling with their dual diagnosis? 

Supporting a loved one struggling with dual diagnosis starts with listening and letting them know they are not alone. Educate yourself about what they are going through so you can better understand what they are experiencing. It is very important to also take care of your own well-being so you can offer consistent, compassionate support.

 

 Does The Meadows provide post-treatment recovery support? 

At The Meadows we know that recovery doesn’t end when treatment does. The Meadows offers several different options, including step-down programs after inpatient treatment through our outpatient programs with locations across the country. Additionally, our alumni program, Onward , is open to any individual who has gone through one of our programs. Onward provides individuals with a supportive community committed to ongoing resources and empowerment, knowing that recovery is a lifelong journey.

 

 Does treatment for dual diagnosis include family involvement and support? 

Family or friend involvement is crucial to the healing process for dual diagnosis. In our programs, we offer a variety of resources for family members , including educational and communication tools, as well as, in some cases, onsite workshops. The decision to attend a family program is guided by several factors, including the length of stay, clinical appropriateness, patient desire, and others. Once admitted, our team members will work with the patient and their family to determine the next steps. 

 

 

 

Traveling for Care?

People from all over choose The Meadows for expert care in trauma, addiction, and mental health, a private environment, and extraordinary recovery outcomes. We ensure every step of your journey is supported so you can focus fully on healing.

 Learn More About Traveling for Care 

 

Regain Control and Find Lasting Recovery

 

You don’t have to manage your mental health disorder and addiction alone. Reach out to our admissions team to explore the right treatment options for you.

 CALL: 928.260.3568 

 GET STARTED Now

<style id='block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css'>
/* Large screens (desktops, 992px and up) */
@media ( min-width: 992px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-large-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Medium screens (tablets, between 768px and 992px) */
@media ( min-width: 768px ) and ( max-width: 991.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-medium-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}

/* Small screens (mobile devices, less than 768px) */
@media ( max-width: 767.98px ) {
	.block-visibility-hide-small-screen {
		display: none !important;
	}
}
/*# sourceURL=block-visibility-screen-size-styles-inline-css */
</style>

.wfea-card__venue_name {
  font-family: 'Lato';
  font-weight: 700;
}

.eaw-time {
  width: 200px! important;
}

/* Custom carousel navigation button styles */
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev,
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  color: inherit;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -40px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -40px !important;
}

:root {
  --wp--custom--carousel-block--navigation-color: white;
}

.align-content-center{
align-content:center;}

@media (max-width: 345px) {
.footer-copyright p {
font-size:10px;
}
}

@media (max-width: 600px) {

.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-prev {
  left: -20px !important;
}
.bcbs-carousel .cb-button-next {
  right: -20px !important;
}

.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-prev,
.ghub-query-slider .ghub-slider-container .ghub-slider-navigation-wrapper .ghub-slider-next {
margin-left: 0;
margin-right: 0;

}

}

.page-id-1186 .is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 24px);
}

.badge-row-hp-cover{
z-index: 2;
position: absolute;
}

.CEO-headshot img{
object-position:top;}

.mtsnb-content{
font-family:"Lato", sans-serif;
}

.page-id-1143 ul{text-transform: none;
}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item{
grid-template-columns: minmax(460px, 1fr) minmax(min-content, 503px) !important;}

body.front-end .wfea-card .wfea-card-item .eaw-content-block .eaw-title{
max-width:40ch !important;}

/* Plus Toggle Style */
.is-style-plus-toggle {
    list-style: none;
    width: 100%;
font-family: "Lato";
padding: 1rem 2rem;
user-select: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] {
padding: 1rem 2rem 4rem;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary {
    list-style: none;
    position: relative;
    padding-right: 2rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    font-size: clamp(24px, 22px + 0.65vw, 32px);
    line-height: clamp(30px, 27px + 0.97vw, 42px);
    cursor: pointer;
text-transform: uppercase;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::-webkit-details-marker {
    display: none;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle > summary::after {
    content: '+';
    position: absolute;
    right: 0;
    top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%);
    width: 2rem;
    height: 2rem;
    font-size: 2rem;
    text-align: center;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    transition: transform 0.3s ease;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] > summary::after {
    content: '×';
}

/* Animations */
@keyframes open-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
}

@keyframes close-details {
    0% { 
        opacity: 1;
        transform: translateY(0);
    }
    100% { 
        opacity: 0;
        transform: translateY(-10px);
    }
}

.is-style-plus-toggle[open] summary ~ * {
    animation: open-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

.is-style-plus-toggle.closing summary ~ * {
    animation: close-details 0.3s ease-in-out;
}

/* Content Styling */
.is-style-plus-toggle .details-content {
    padding: 1rem 0;
}
:root :where(.wp-block-button .wp-block-button__link){background-color: var(--wp--preset--color--white);border-radius: 0;border-top-width: 1px;border-top-style: solid;border-right-width: 1px;border-right-style: solid;border-bottom-width: 1px;border-bottom-style: solid;border-left-width: 1px;border-left-style: solid;color: var(--wp--preset--color--purple-brand);font-family: var(--wp--preset--font-family--open-sans);font-size: var(--wp--preset--font-size--link);font-style: normal;font-weight: 700;letter-spacing: 1.6px;line-height: 1;}
:root :where(.wp-block-button .wp-block-button__link a:where(:not(.wp-element-button))){color: var(--wp--preset--color--purple-brand);}
:root :where(p){font-family: var(--wp--preset--font-family--lato);font-size: var(--wp--preset--font-size--body);font-weight: 300;letter-spacing: 0.95px;line-height: 34px;}
:root :where(p a:where(:not(.wp-element-button))){text-decoration: none;}
:root :where(p a:where(:not(.wp-element-button)):hover){text-decoration: underline;}
:root :where(.wp-block-list){font-size: clamp(14px, 0.875rem + ((1vw - 3.2px) * 0.469), 20px);letter-spacing: 0.95px;line-height: 1.8;}
:root :where(.wp-block-navigation){font-size: var(--wp--preset--font-size--small);}
:root :where(.wp-block-navigation a:where(:not(.wp-element-button))){text-decoration: none;}
:root :where(.wp-block-navigation a:where(:not(.wp-element-button)):hover){text-decoration: underline;}
:root :where(.wp-block-navigation a:where(:not(.wp-element-button)):focus){text-decoration: underline dashed;}
:root :where(.wp-block-navigation a:where(:not(.wp-element-button)):active){text-decoration: none;}
:root :where(.wp-block-quote){border-width: 0;padding-right: 0;padding-left: 0;}
:root :where(.wp-block-quote cite){font-size: var(--wp--preset--font-size--small);font-style: normal;}
/*# sourceURL=global-styles-inline-css */

<style id='wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css'>

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text {
			border: 0;
			clip-path: inset(50%);
			height: 1px;
			margin: -1px;
			overflow: hidden;
			padding: 0;
			position: absolute !important;
			width: 1px;
			word-wrap: normal !important;
		}

		.skip-link.screen-reader-text:focus {
			background-color: #eee;
			clip-path: none;
			color: #444;
			display: block;
			font-size: 1em;
			height: auto;
			left: 5px;
			line-height: normal;
			padding: 15px 23px 14px;
			text-decoration: none;
			top: 5px;
			width: auto;
			z-index: 100000;
		}
/*# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-inline-css */
</style>

<h1 class="wp-block-heading is-style-pulse-in-h1 has-heading-1-font-size">Dual Diagnosis Treatment</h1>



<div class="wp-block-group is-content-justification-left is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-74cbbf86 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<p class="has-hero-subtitle-font-size" style="font-style:normal;font-weight:400">Take control of your mental health and addiction with comprehensive care for co-occurring conditions.</p>



<div class="wp-block-buttons is-layout-flex wp-block-buttons-is-layout-flex">
<div class="wp-block-button is-style-outline-chalk"><a class="wp-block-button__link wp-element-button" href="https://www.themeadows.com/contact/">Get Started</a></div>
</div>
</div>



<div style="height:65px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>
</div>
</div>
</div></div>
</div></div>



<div class="wp-block-group alignwide has-chalk-light-background-color has-background is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-88c27b7c wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">
<div class="wp-block-group is-style-force-left-mobile has-stark-color has-text-color is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-e26a7a31 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0">
<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-749858fd wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading has-text-align-center">Dual Diagnosis: Treating Addiction and Mental Health Together</h2>
</div>



<hr class="wp-block-separator has-text-color has-desert-color has-alpha-channel-opacity has-desert-background-color has-background is-style-narrowed"/>



<p class="has-text-align-center">At our dual diagnosis treatment facilities, we offer a supportive environment to help you address both mental health disorders and substance use issues. Our programs are designed to treat both conditions simultaneously, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. With our integrated approach, you’ll develop the tools to manage both mental health challenges and addiction, creating a more stable and fulfilling future.</p>
</div>
</div>



<div class="wp-block-columns fifty-fifty is-style-stack-backwards has-plum-medium-color has-chalk-background-color has-text-color has-background has-link-color wp-elements-5dc900fcff096083b923fcbd291b5de7 is-layout-flex wp-container-core-columns-is-layout-b43e120a wp-block-columns-is-layout-flex" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0">
<div class="wp-block-column is-vertically-aligned-center fifty-fifty-text-content is-layout-flow wp-block-column-is-layout-flow" style="padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0;flex-basis:50%">
<div class="wp-block-group is-style-default is-content-justification-right is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-107e3f96 wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">
<div class="wp-block-group is-layout-constrained wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Personalized Care, Lasting Recovery</h2>



<p>Our approach to dual diagnosis treatment focuses on creating personalized care plans that address both your mental health disorder and addiction. We treat the whole person, recognizing how mental health and addiction influence one another. This ensures lasting recovery by addressing the root causes of both conditions and equipping you with the tools to manage them effectively.</p>
</div>


</div>

<div class="wp-block-query is-layout-flow wp-block-query-is-layout-flow"><ul id="6" class="wp-block-post-template is-layout-flow wp-block-post-template-is-layout-flow"><li class="wp-block-post post-8139 faq type-faq status-publish hentry faq-category-what-we-treat">

<h2 class="wp-block-heading" style="text-transform:none">Dual Diagnosis Treatment FAQ</h2>


<div style="text-transform:none;" class="entry-content wp-block-post-content is-layout-flow wp-block-post-content-is-layout-flow">
<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What is dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p><a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/dual-diagnosis/">Dual Diagnosis</a> is the <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/blog/why-are-co-occurring-disorders-so-common/">co-occurring presence of both a substance use disorder and a mental health condition</a>. This might include combinations like depression and alcohol use disorder, anxiety and prescription drug misuse, or <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/blog/30-things-you-need-to-know-about-trauma-and-ptsd-ptsd-awareness-month/">PTSD</a> and opioid addiction. These conditions often interact and reinforce one another, making professional treatment essential. </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Which typically comes first – mental health concerns or substance use?</summary>
<p>It varies from person to person. Some individuals use substances to cope with underlying <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/mental-health/">mental health</a> struggles, while others develop mental health symptoms as a result of prolonged substance use. The most important thing is recognizing the connection and ensuring both are addressed in treatment for long-term healing and recovery.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Can you treat mental health and substance use at the same time?</summary>
<p>Absolutely. Treating both conditions simultaneously, rather than sequentially, improves outcomes and reduces the risk of relapse. At The Meadows, our programs are designed to treat both conditions at the same time, ensuring that you regain control over your life and find long-term recovery. </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>How do I know if I or a loved one is struggling with addiction and mental health simultaneously?</summary>
<p>If you notice persistent emotional distress alongside substance use, such as mood swings, depression, anxiety, isolation, or impulsive behavior, it may be a dual diagnosis. Warning signs also include using substances to cope with emotions, needing more to achieve the same effect, or being unable to stop despite negative consequences. To learn more and see if your loved one could be struggling with a dual diagnosis, call our team at <a href="tel:928.260.3568">928.260.3568.</a></p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What are the symptoms and signs of someone struggling with a dual diagnosis? </summary>
<p>A proper evaluation is important to understand the full picture; however, common signs in those struggling with a dual diagnosis include: </p>



<p>Denial or secrecy about substance use </p>



<p>Changes in mood or behavior (irritability, anxiety, depression)</p>



<p>Difficulty managing daily responsibilities </p>



<p>Withdrawing from loved ones </p>



<p>Substance use that interferes with relationships or work </p>



<p>Trouble sleeping or concentrating </p>



<p>Risk-taking or impulsive behavior </p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>What kind of treatment works best for dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p>The most effective treatment for dual diagnosis can vary from person to person, but integrated care that addresses both the mental health condition and the substance use disorder is important. <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/inpatient/">Inpatient</a>, <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/outpatient/">outpatient</a>, and <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/workshops/">workshops</a> are all different programs that can help in the recovery of dual diagnosis.  A <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/treatment/types-of-therapy/">blend of therapies</a> is often best because it treats the whole person, addressing emotional, physical, and behavioral needs together for more lasting recovery.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>How can I support a loved one struggling with their dual diagnosis?</summary>
<p>Supporting <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/guidance-for-families-and-loved-ones/">a loved one</a> struggling with dual diagnosis starts with listening and letting them know they are not alone. Educate yourself about what they are going through so you can better understand what they are experiencing. It is very important to also take care of your own well-being so you can offer consistent, compassionate support.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Does The Meadows provide post-treatment recovery support? </summary>
<p>At The Meadows we know that recovery doesn’t end when treatment does. The Meadows offers several different options, including step-down programs after <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/inpatient/">inpatient treatment</a> through our <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/outpatient/">outpatient programs</a> with locations across the country. Additionally, our alumni program, <a href="https://mbhonward.com/">Onward</a>, is open to any individual who has gone through one of our programs. Onward provides individuals with a supportive community committed to ongoing resources and empowerment, knowing that recovery is a lifelong journey.</p>
</details>



<details class="wp-block-details is-style-faq-item is-layout-flow wp-block-details-is-layout-flow"><summary>Does treatment for dual diagnosis include family involvement and support? </summary>
<p>Family or friend involvement is crucial to the healing process for dual diagnosis. In our programs, we offer a variety of <a href="https://www.themeadows.com/guidance-for-families-and-loved-ones/">resources for family members</a>, including educational and communication tools, as well as, in some cases, onsite workshops. The decision to attend a family program is guided by several factors, including the length of stay, clinical appropriateness, patient desire, and others. Once admitted, our team members will work with the patient and their family to determine the next steps. </p>
</details>
</div>
</li></ul>

</div>

</div>

<div class="wp-block-group is-style-default is-content-justification-left is-layout-constrained wp-container-core-group-is-layout-4522ad1d wp-block-group-is-layout-constrained" style="margin-top:0;margin-bottom:0;padding-top:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-right:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90);padding-bottom:var(--wp--preset--spacing--70);padding-left:var(--wp--preset--spacing--90)">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Traveling for Care?</h2>



<p>People from all over choose The Meadows for expert care in trauma, addiction, and mental health, a private environment, and extraordinary recovery outcomes. We ensure every step of your journey is supported so you can focus fully on healing.</p>

<script id="wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after">
	( function() {
		var skipLinkTarget = document.querySelector( 'main' ),
			sibling,
			skipLinkTargetID,
			skipLink;

		// Early exit if a skip-link target can't be located.
		if ( ! skipLinkTarget ) {
			return;
		}

		/*
		 * Get the site wrapper.
		 * The skip-link will be injected in the beginning of it.
		 */
		sibling = document.querySelector( '.wp-site-blocks' );

		// Early exit if the root element was not found.
		if ( ! sibling ) {
			return;
		}

		// Get the skip-link target's ID, and generate one if it doesn't exist.
		skipLinkTargetID = skipLinkTarget.id;
		if ( ! skipLinkTargetID ) {
			skipLinkTargetID = 'wp--skip-link--target';
			skipLinkTarget.id = skipLinkTargetID;
		}

		// Create the skip link.
		skipLink = document.createElement( 'a' );
		skipLink.classList.add( 'skip-link', 'screen-reader-text' );
		skipLink.id = 'wp-skip-link';
		skipLink.href = '#' + skipLinkTargetID;
		skipLink.innerText = 'Skip to content';

		// Inject the skip link.
		sibling.parentElement.insertBefore( skipLink, sibling );
	}() );
	
//# sourceURL=wp-block-template-skip-link-js-after
</script>
